subject: Re: Warp 1.0.7 Crashing on Particle Picking
body:
Sorry, that command should have included '--dont_flatten_spectrum'. In case 
you would like to try that tool for actual denoising in the future, here is 
further documentation (site is back up!): 
http://www.warpem.com/warp/?page_id=389

On Thursday, November 14, 2019 at 3:33:42 PM UTC+1, Kendra wrote:
>
> Hi Dimitry,
>
> This machine is one of our two dedicated Warp servers, so there aren't any 
> other programs on the computer (aside from default Windows programs) and 
> Warp.  As a result, I wouldn't expect there to be another cuDNN library 
> somewhere.  Is there a way for me to check for this?
>
> I removed 1.0.6 (using the installer "Remove" option + deleting the 
> installation directory).  I then installed 1.0.7 using the Windows 
> installer.  I checked $PATH and see the installation directory has been 
> added (see attached screenshot).  I then executed the Noise2Map command you 
> requested on a map of apoferritin from the EMDB and got the following error:
>
> Unhandled Exception: System.Exception: Flattening requested, but pixel 
> size not specified.
>       at Noise2Map.Program.Main(String[] args)
>
> I also got a pop-up Windows window saying Noise2Map has stopped 
> working..etc (see attached screenshot).  The header of the .mrc files does 
> have the pixel spacing specified.  Should I have provided it in another way?
>
> As a side note, I realised I may have not been totally clear before, but 
> warpem.com seems to be down in general (not from the 1.0.8 pop-up link). 
>
> Thank you for your help!
>
> Cheers,
> Kendra
>
>
>
> On Thursday, November 14, 2019 at 2:04:20 PM UTC+1, Dimitry Tegunov wrote:
>>
>> Hi Kendra,
>>
>> is there anything else using TensorFlow or GPU-accelerated deep learning 
>> in general on that system? I have the same driver version on my system, so 
>> that definitely works. It sounds like only the TensorFlow part of Warp 
>> isn't working. TF requires the cuDNN library for GPU acceleration, and the 
>> correct version is shipped with Warp. Perhaps there is a different version 
>> installed somewhere that takes priority?
>>
>> Here is something that might provide more diagnostic output: With Warp 
>> 1.0.7 installed (the installer should add the installation directory to the 
>> $PATH variable -- if it isn't there, please add it manually), create 2 
>> folders named 'odd' and 'even', put the same (small) 3D map in MRC format 
>> in both, and execute 'Noise2Map --observation1 odd --observation2 even' in 
>> the folder containing those 2 folders. See if it crashes and writes out 
>> something helpful when it tries to load the TF model.
>>
>> 1.0.8 was an unfortunate solution to the problem that some 1.0.7 beta 
>> versions already had the version specified as 1.0.7 and thus wouldn't have 
>> notified the user about the final 1.0.7 update :-) I forgot it would show 
>> up in that message.
>>
>> Cheers,
>> Dimitry
>>
>> On Thursday, November 14, 2019 at 12:32:44 PM UTC+1, Kendra wrote:
>>>
>>> Hi Dimitry,
>>>
>>> We removed 1.0.7 and installed 1.0.6. We still get the same type of 
>>> Windows crash error, again only when particle picking is selected.  Our 
>>> current NVIDIA driver version is 26.21.14.4112 (our IT person took care of 
>>> the OS install/driver updates/etc.).  Do we need a different version? 
>>>
>>> As a side note, upon opening 1.0.6, it claims that a new version, 1.0.8, 
>>> is available (see screenshot attached).  I cannot find evidence of a 1.0.8 
>>> on Github and it also seems like the warpem.com site is down (an "Error 
>>> establishing a database connection").  Is this just a typo? :)
>>>
>>> Thank you for your help!
>>>
>>> Cheers,
>>> Kendra
>>>
>>>
>>>
>>> On Sunday, November 10, 2019 at 9:26:46 PM UTC+1, Dimitry Tegunov wrote:
>>>>
>>>> Hi Kendra,
>>>>
>>>> did you try 1.0.6 before? That's when the switch to a new CUDA version 
>>>> happened, requiring a GPU driver update. Though if you reinstalled the 
>>>> whole OS, it sounds like you installed the latest drivers as well. Could 
>>>> you please try removing 1.0.7 and installing 1.0.6 instead to check if that 
>>>> works at all?
>>>>
>>>> Cheers,
>>>> Dimitry
>>>>
>>>>
>>>> On Friday, November 8, 2019 at 7:12:03 PM UTC+1, Kendra wrote:
>>>>>
>>>>> Hi Everyone,
>>>>>
>>>>> We had Warp 1.0.5 successfully running on two workstations.  We have 
>>>>> tried upgrading one of the workstations to Warp 1.0.7 but have been unable 
>>>>> to successfully run particle picking.  Initially we installed 1.0.7 and it 
>>>>> would run as expected with no particle picking.  Once we selected particle 
>>>>> picking, it would suddenly close without providing any errors. At the same 
>>>>> time the computer started running very slowly and since we weren't sure if 
>>>>> maybe the computer was at fault, we did a clean install of the operating 
>>>>> system.  Afterwards, we installed 1.0.7 again and the computer was running 
>>>>> as expected, but the program still crashes if we include the particle 
>>>>> picking option.  Now, however, it provides a generic error message (see 
>>>>> attached PNG), but lastcrash.txt doesn't show anything related to the 
>>>>> crash.  
>>>>>
>>>>> Our set-up is as follows:
>>>>> Windows 10 Pro
>>>>> 96 GB RAM - this is on the low side, but was sufficient for 1.0.5
>>>>> 2 x Intel Xenon E5 3.3 GHz processors
>>>>> 3 x GTX 1080 Ti with 11 GB memory
>>>>>
>>>>> Is there a reason why 1.0.7 would fail at the particle picking step 
>>>>> when 1.0.5 did not, given our configuration?  We would like to know if this 
>>>>> is something we can fix by changing something or if this is a more general 
>>>>> issue with 1.0.7.
>>>>>
>>>>> Thanks for any help you can give!
>>>>>
>>>>> Sincerely,
>>>>> Kendra Leigh
>>>>>
>>>>>
>>>>>
>>>>>
